meta.architecture: 3 input sheets → intermediate computation sheets → summary P&L. Branch count is the master multiplier: all revenues and all costs scale with it. Columns = time periods (years Y1-Y6). Rows = line items.

branches (Branch!E).note: Changing branch count scales ALL revenue and ALL costs simultaneously

product qty formula (A.I Revenue Streams.note: Changes volume assumption for that product

opex cost/month/branch (A.IIOPEX!I col).note: Scales with branch count. Main driver of cost structure

revenue_streams.description: Product-level revenue assumptions. Each product has a qty/branch formula, sale price, and year-over-year growth rates.
revenue_streams.branches_note: H7 = number of branches. All qty formulas multiply by this.

Radiology-CT.product: CT Angio of Aorta and Coronaries
CT Joints (Knee, Shoulder and others) with 3D

branch_rollout.description: Monthly branch count and subscription volumes. Drives scale multipliers in all revenue/cost computations.

key_inputs.marketplace_subs_col: J — Marketplace subscriptions (user enters from month 7)

opex.description: Operating cost assumptions per cost category. Costs are per branch per month and scale with branch count.
opex.cost_structure_note: All costs multiply by branch count. Scaling: cost × branches × growth_factor
